key_insight: MAGE improves gastric adenoma-vs-carcinoma classification by distilling structural knowledge from a masked, color-suppressed expert into a full-image model so predictions rely more on morphology than spurious visual cues.
clinical_relevance: This could make endoscopic classification more reliable and deployable without needing annotation masks at inference, supporting faster and more interpretable decision support.
method: Train an auxiliary local expert on masked achromatic neoplasm views to emphasize shape and structure.
Distill both class logits and spatial attention maps from the expert to the main whole-image branch.
Use full white-light endoscopy images at inference with no mask requirement.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that experiments on a clinical gastric endoscopy dataset showed significantly better performance than detector-based methods like YOLO and classifier-based methods like Swin-Transformer, with interpretable attention maps.
watch_item: Performance and generalizability may depend on the quality of the training masks and the source dataset, so external validation across sites and imaging conditions is important.
paper_title: MAGE: Color-Invariant and Spatial Knowledge Distillation for Gastric Neoplasm Classification
